Ad Text:
At VALAI, we build technology that turns property data into real action. Our vision is to transform how property is valued to drive a zero-carbon economy, where green finance accelerates the energy transition. Our platform serves major enterprise clients across banking, property, energy, insurance, and local government.

We are at a critical stage of platform growth, preparing several products and integrations for enterprise production deployment. We need a reliable, high-output Full Stack Engineer to jump into the engine room and build the API connections and data pipelines that make it all happen.

The Role

This isn't a role for someone who just wants to push pixels. You will be the API and Data Pipeline specialist within a lean, fast-moving engineering team.

Day-to-day, you will be building the integrations that power VALAI’s scoring and recommendations engine, supporting third-party enterprise feeds, and hardening our platform for production-grade reliability and security. You'll pick up Jira tasks across the stack, contribute to time-bound sprint delivery, and be a reliable pair of hands when P1 incidents need fast triage.

What you will actually do:

Design & Build: Develop and maintain RESTful APIs and integration connectors for our enterprise partners using Python and FastAPI.

Data Pipelines: Build robust data ingestion pipelines that can reliably process property, energy, and external partner data at scale (supporting SFTP, API, CSV, and webhooks).

Enterprise Hardening: Support the readiness of VALAI products for deployment. This means environment separation, security hardening, applying least-privilege access patterns, and managing secrets.

DevOps & Stability: Maintain CI/CD pipelines via GitHub Actions, automate deployment workflows, and use CloudWatch to proactively monitor system performance.

Incident Response: Step up to triage and resolve Priority 1 incidents across the infrastructure, application, and data layers to keep the platform stable.

Data Quality: Support data schema mapping, validation, transformation, and rigorous quality checks across pipeline stages.

What You Bring (The Non-Negotiables)

You are someone who takes initiative and moves work forward without needing to be closely managed. You care about doing things properly, not just quickly, so we aren't compounding technical debt.

Strong, hands-on Python experience, specifically using FastAPI for building and maintaining production APIs.

Deep experience designing and managing integrations (REST APIs, SFTP, webhooks, scheduled imports).

Proven ability to build and maintain data pipelines (ingestion, transformation, validation, schema mapping).

Real confidence with AWS core services: ECS/Fargate, API Gateway, RDS/Postgres, ALB, WAF, CloudWatch, S3, and IAM.

Experience with CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions or similar).

Strong capability in triaging and resolving production incidents.

Clear communication skills to document APIs, pipeline designs, and runbooks for the rest of the team.

Highly Regarded (Bonus Points):

Front-end experience in React (TypeScript/Next.js).

Experience with BigQuery, dbt, Airbyte, or similar data warehouse tooling.

Familiarity with infrastructure as code (Terraform) or caching patterns (Redis).

Experience working on white-labelled/multi-tenant enterprise platforms or ISO 27001 compliance (Vanta).

The Details

Remuneration: contact ope•••••••@valai.com.au

Engagement: Full-time (37.5 hours per week).

Location: Australia preferred (Remote / Hybrid flexibility).

Reports to: CEO and Technical Lead.

How to Apply

Please submit your CV and a short, cover note outlining:

Your hands-on experience with Python, FastAPI, and AWS.

A brief example of a complex API integration or data pipeline you’ve built or scaled.

Why you want to use your engineering skills in the climate/property tech space with VALAI.

Technical Lead (AI-First, Climate Tech)

At VALAI, we build technology that turns property data into real action—real savings, real electrification, and real climate resilience. Our data intelligence powers Home Efficiency Australia and supports national banking, insurance, property, energy, and government partners.




We are scaling quickly and need a Technical Lead to shape and drive technology delivery across our residential, commercial, energy, water, and climate resilience product suite. If you need heavy process and clear structure before you can act, this is probably not the right role. If you thrive on meaningful problems, lean teams, and the freedom to shape how things are built, you will love it here.




The Role

This is a leadership role first, focused heavily on architecture decisions, delivery velocity, engineering standards, AI adoption, and team capability. Ambiguity is not an obstacle here—it is the environment. You will be expected to navigate it, make confident decisions with incomplete information, and keep delivery moving.




What you will actually do:

Own the technical direction across the product platform, including architecture decisions and delivery sequencing.




Scope technical work, estimate effort, and communicate trade-offs clearly to product and leadership.




Lead the design and delivery of AI-powered product features, such as LLM integrations, intelligent data processing, and automated workflows.




Drive the adoption of AI-assisted development tools (like GitHub Copilot, Cursor, or Claude) as standard practice across the team.




Guide front-end (React, Next.js) and backend (Python, FastAPI, TypeScript, NestJS) delivery to ensure quality and performance.




Support cloud infrastructure decisions across AWS, oversee CI/CD pipelines, and manage release automation.




Support our security posture by applying access controls and maintaining ISO 27001-aligned controls using tools like Vanta.




Manage contractors, remote contributors, and improve code review practices across the team.




What You Bring

The ideal candidate has earned their credibility by building things, but is now ready to step up and own how a team builds.




Proven experience leading technical delivery in a scale-up or fast-moving product environment.




Deep, practical experience working with AI/LLM APIs as a core engineering capability, not just a side project.




A strong, hands-on background across front-end development, backend development, and cloud infrastructure.




A solid understanding of API design, secure coding, CI/CD pipelines, and database management.




The ability to prototype quickly with AI tools and make sound build-versus-buy-versus-integrate decisions.




Strong communication skills to keep technical and non-technical stakeholders perfectly aligned.




Highly Regarded:

Experience with infrastructure as code (Kubernetes, Terraform) or data tools (BigQuery, Snowflake, dbt).




Experience with white-labelled platforms, enterprise partner integrations, or ISO 27001 compliance environments.




A background in climate tech, energy, property, insurance, or banking.




The Details

Engagement: Full-time, 12-month contract with flexible hours.

Location: Hybrid, with Adelaide preferred.

Probation: 12 weeks.

For Salary info contact ope•••••••@valai.com.au




How to Apply

Please submit your CV along with a short cover note. In your cover note, briefly outline:




Your technical leadership experience and the types of products you have led.




How you practically use AI in your development tooling and product features.




An example of how you have navigated significant ambiguity in a scale-up environment.




A quick summary of your hands-on background across the stack, and why you want to build in the climate/property tech space.

Join hummgroup, one of Australasia's most successful and enduring non-bank financial services institutions. We specialise in fast, easy finance with a market-leading range of business asset finance, credit cards and point of sale payment plan solutions designed around the core needs of today's businesses and retail consumers. Come join our team and be a part of our next chapter of innovation and growth.


What's so exciting about this role?

This is a high‑impact Data Engineering role where you'll work on strategic initiatives that shape how data is used, governed and trusted across the organisation. You'll design and deliver scalable data solutions, partner closely with governance, security and platform teams, and play a key role in lifting data quality and compliance. As the business matures, you'll also enable advanced analytics, AI and machine learning by building robust data products, pipelines and lineage that power future insights.

What Does An Average Day Look Like?

No day is average at humm but here's an idea of what you can expect to do in this role:



Identify, document and remediate non‑compliant or sensitive data across data repositories, including retention breaches and masking requirements.
Partner with Data Governance and Security teams to translate policy into scalable technical controls (e.g. PII detection, data aging, masking and access controls).
Design, implement and automate compliant data solutions that improve data quality, reliability and processing efficiency across cloud and on‑prem environments.
Ensure performance, scalability and monitoring of data solutions, proactively identifying and resolving data issues or bottlenecks.
Contribute to best practices, documentation and cross‑functional collaboration that strengthen data governance, security and compliance culture.


Who will thrive in this role?

This role is ideal for someone with a strong background in cloud data engineering, data pipeline development and data modelling, particularly in a complex financial services data environment.

We're also looking for someone who brings:


3+ years' experience in data engineering, with hands‑on expertise across modern cloud data platforms (e.g. Snowflake, Azure, AWS).
Strong technical skills in data pipelines and transformations, including Azure Data Factory, DBT Cloud, SQL, and Python or similar scripting languages.
Experience designing scalable, performant data solutions, with knowledge of orchestration tools, CI/CD pipelines and data platform optimisation.
Solid understanding of data governance, security and compliance best practices, ideally within complex or regulated environments.
We operate in a dynamic, fast-paced environment and seek engineers who can effectively manage multiple priorities, adapt quickly to changing requirements, and transition seamlessly between tasks while maintaining a high standard of quality and attention to detail.
Comfortable working in agile, cross‑functional teams; experience with financial services, analytics or marketing data is advantageous.
Interest in emerging capabilities such as streaming data (Kafka/Kinesis) and supporting AI/ML workloads as data platforms evolve.


Working at hummgroup

We embrace flexible working arrangements, ensuring a genuine work-life balance and a fun, vibrant inclusive culture that embraces diverse perspectives and values the unique contributions of each of our team through our behaviours Curious - Adaptable - Tenacious - Accountable - Respectful.

We invite candidates of all gender expressions, ethnicities, ages, and other culturally diverse groups to apply. We are committed to making reasonable adjustments to provide a positive, barrier-free recruitment process and welcoming and supportive environment where all our people can thrive.

Data Science

software engineering tools and workflows

This national cultural institution could be described as the memory of our nation and is responsible for preserving and enabling access to some of Australia’s most important records.

They are in the midst of a significant, multi-year transformation program focused on digitising collections, modernising legacy platforms, and repositioning data as a core strategic asset across the organisation.

The program is led by the CIO who has a track record of delivery success through building great teams.

The Opportunity

This is an opportunity to join a genuinely meaningful data platform build.

The Unified Data View project is focused on designing and delivering a centralised data capability that brings together fragmented, complex collection metadata into a single, trusted and consumable layer for analytics, reporting, and operational decision-making.

You’ll sit within a small, 7 person delivery team and play a key role in shaping how data is structured, governed, and used across the organisation.

What makes this interesting:

Greenfield-style build of an enterprise data platform sitting across deeply entrenched legacy systems
Working with highly complex, interrelated metadata where structure, lineage and meaning matter as much as the data itself
The Role 

This is a hands-on data engineering role with architectural influence. It’s not   just about building or optimising pipelines but also helping define how the data environment should work.

Design and evolve a scalable data warehouse architecture in Microsoft Fabric aligned to business rules and archival control models
Build robust ETL/ELT pipelines to ingest, transform and structure data from multiple complex source systems
Own source-to-target mapping, data modelling, and lineage to ensure transparency and trust in the data
Embed data quality, validation and governance frameworks into the platform from the ground up
The Ideal Person

This suits someone who’s comfortable working in complex, messy data environments and building structure where it doesn’t currently exist.

You’re experienced at building pipelines and also  understand data end-to-end how it’s modelled, governed, and consumed  and you’re just as comfortable in the detail as you are stepping back to understand how it needs to hold together and be used.
 

Strong experience designing and delivering data platforms in Azure/Microsoft Fabric environments
Deep capability in ETL/ELT development using Python (including PySpark/Pandas) and modern data engineering practices
Advanced Power BI capability, including data modelling, DAX, and shaping data for analytical consumption
You’ll also bring a level of maturity around documentation, lineage, and governance understanding that in this environment, clarity and traceability are critical.

Due to the nature of the project Australia Citizenship and Baseline or above is mandatory.
 
Salary / Rate

Expected rate range $140/hr - $160/hr
Labour-hire contract

About Cell-Media

Cell-Media is a world-leading eLearning design and development company. We build custom digital training for some of the largest companies on the planet — BHP, Rio Tinto, Roy Hill, Newmont, Glencore, Talison Lithium, South32, Shell, and BP among them. Our core markets are Mining & Resources and Oil & Gas, and every year we deliver engaging content to thousands of employees, contractors, and visitors across the globe.

With 20+ eLearning designers, a dedicated scripting team, an in-house development team, and offices across Australia, Singapore, and Malaysia, we're investing in the next chapter of our scripting capability — one that pairs solid writing craft with modern AI workflows.

About the Role

We're hiring a Technical Content Writer on a 12-month contract, with a clear path to permanent employment for the right person.

You'll turn, technical training material — operating procedures, safety standards, equipment manuals, regulatory content — into eLearning scripts people actually want to engage with. You'll work alongside client stakeholders, subject matter experts (SMEs), instructional designers, and developers, with AI tools at your fingertips to speed up the work.

This is a role for someone who's technology-curious, willing and writes well. If you're comfortable with AI tools, organised, and keen to grow, we want to hear from you.

What you'll do

AI-assisted script development. Use AI tools as drafting and QA partners throughout the scriptwriting process. This includes generating first-draft narration from SME source material (PPTX, technical documents, regulatory references), restructuring content for instructional flow, refining tone for frontline audiences, and running quality checks against approved scripts. For security-sensitive content, our in-house technical scripting LLM keeps client material inside Cell-Media's controlled environment. AI tools speed up drafting and review — they don't replace SME input or final editorial judgement.

Review client-supplied training materials (PPTX, PDF, Word, SOPs, technical drawings) and scope them into structured eLearning scripts.

Write engaging, narrative-driven scripts that include interactive learning elements, scenarios, and multimedia cues.

Work with client stakeholders and SMEs to confirm technical accuracy and finalise scripts for production.

Liaise with audio vendors to brief voice talent and manage recordings, including multi-language productions.

Keep script libraries, version control, and client documents organised for ongoing programs.

Hand over polished scripts to designers and developers so narration, on-screen content, and assets come together cleanly.

Report into the project management team to track deadlines and flag risks early.

Help improve our AI-assisted scripting workflows as the tooling evolves.

What we're looking for

A few years' experience writing instructional, training, technical, or similar content.

Hands-on experience using AI writing tools (ChatGPT, Claude, Copilot, Gemini, or similar) and genuine curiosity about where the technology is heading.

Good editorial instincts: you can take an AI draft and turn it into something accurate, clear, and useful for a learner.

The ability to pick up technical subject matter quickly and explain it simply.

Clear written and interpersonal communication — you'll be talking to engineers, safety leads, and project managers.

Organised, with the ability to juggle multiple clients, projects, and script versions.

A self-starter who can manage their own workload and raise problems early.

Comfortable working within established systems, templates, and quality standards.

Nice to have

Exposure to mining, resources, oil & gas, or another technical/regulated sector.

Familiarity with instructional design frameworks (ADDIE, SAM) or eLearning tools (Articulate, Storyline, Rise).

Experience briefing voice talent or managing audio production.

Understanding of adult learning principles.

Where you'll work

Our brand-new St Georges Terrace office sits right on Perth's most iconic street, with floor-to-ceiling views over the Terrace and the city skyline. It's a modern, light-filled space designed for collaboration — the kind of office you actually look forward to coming into. You'll be surrounded by designers, developers, and writers working on real projects for real clients, with everything you need to do your best work close at hand.

Why join us

Real career path: 12-month contract with a genuine view to permanent employment.

Work with major clients across Mining & Resources and Oil & Gas.

Be part of a team building AI-augmented workflows — including our own in-house technical scripting LLM.

Brand-new St Georges Terrace office overlooking the city — modern, collaborative, and a genuinely great place to work.

Supportive leadership and a tight-knit team that takes the craft seriously.

Competitive salary: $80,000 – $95,000 per year, depending on experience.

If you're a clear writer who's curious about AI and keen to grow into technical content, we'd love to hear from you.

Apply now to join Cell-Media.

AI Model Engineer
Generative HDL & Agentic Design Systems

Cadence Connect has been exclusively retained to appoint an AI Model Engineer for a venture-backed deep tech company building AI-native infrastructure for chip design.

This is core model ownership, not a wrapper around someone else’s API.
You will build, train, tune and productise the AI systems that generate, refactor and debug real Verilog/SystemVerilog RTL used in real engineering workflows.

The Role

You will be responsible for the intelligence layer powering a generative chip design platform. In collaboration with the EDA engineers who own simulation and toolchains, you own how the model is trained on those signals, how it learns, and how it improves engineering outcomes over time. This is model AND product. If it doesn’t improve compile rates, test pass rates or repair success, it won't fly.

What will you be doing? Core Model Capability

Generate clean, maintainable RTL from specifications
Refactor and incrementally edit existing RTL without destructive rewrites
Preserve ports, signal widths and interface consistency
Implement supervised fine-tuning, iterative training cycles and preference-based optimisation
Agentic Workflows

Move beyond single-shot generation by building...

Spec decomposition and structured outputs
Review → critic → repair loops
Safe edit patterns that minimise unnecessary diffs
Predictable behaviours engineers can trust, reinforced through continuous model training
Data & Evaluation

Curate high-quality spec-to-RTL and repair datasets to support robust model training
Design synthetic tasks with controlled bug injection
Implement compile/test-based quality gates
Build benchmarks tracking compile success, test pass rates and regressions to inform ongoing training improvements
Production Readiness

Improve latency and responsiveness
Contribute to caching, quantisation and versioning strategies
Monitor model quality, training drift and failure modes, not just uptime
What we are looking for...
2–5 years in ML engineering, applied research or LLM product development
Hands-on experience training and fine-tuning LLMs, particularly for structured or code generation tasks
Experience building evaluation suites and regression tracking
Ability to read/write basic Verilog/SystemVerilog
Strong Python (PyTorch preferred)
Experience integrating LLMs into multi-step or agent-based workflows
Strong engineering discipline and clear communication
What does success in this position look like?
Measurable gains in HDL generation and repair reliability
Improved structured outputs and interface consistency
Repeatable model training and evaluation cycles without regressions
A dependable, continuously improving AI foundation ready to scale
Hybrid working. Australia based. Travel as required.

This is an exceptional opportunity for a passionate and dedicated AI Model specialist.

THE OPPORTUNITY

Are you an Engineer who thrives on solving "invisible" problems? Our client is a premier electromagnetic shielding specialist protecting mission critical infrastructure for the Defence, Medical, and Scientific sectors.

We aren't just looking for an employee; we are looking for a successor. You will be mentored directly by the Senior Engineer, an industry icon with over 30 years of experience. This role is a long term commitment featuring a structured training path designed to transform a sharp engineer into a specialised industry leader.




THE ROLE

We believe you can’t design a solution if you don't know how it’s built. Your journey begins with a “hands on" immersion:

Spend significant time on-site observing the installation of copper enclosures and modular rooms. You will learn to identify physical deficiencies before you ever certify an installation.
Learn to translate complex architectural drawings into sophisticated shielding reports for hospitals, data centers, and government "quiet areas."
Work with "noise-canceling" active cancellation systems and perform theoretical simulations using MATLAB, MathCAD, or Mathematica.
 You won’t be stuck behind a desk. You will spend 20–50% of your time in the field, from hospital wings to rural paddocks, using generators and specialised equipment to measure ambient EMF.
Travel locally, interstate, and occasionally overseas to certify installations and consult with international principals.




ABOUT YOU

You are an Australian Citizen (essential for our military and government contracts).
Hold a degree in Electrical or Electronics Engineering.
You have the "math brain" for complex calculations but the practical grit to navigate a dirty, uncontrolled building site.
You take pride in the "integrity" of your data and are looking for a "forever" role where you can build a highly specialised career.
Proven ability to interpret building layouts and electrical distribution schematics.




THE BENEFITS

This is a rare 5 year growth plan specifically designed to prepare you to take over the Senior Engineer’s desk. We are investing in the future leader of our engineering department. 

You will have direct access to 30 years of specialised IP and “on the tools" training that you cannot get in a classroom.
One day you’re testing electron microscope rooms at a university; the next, you’re troubleshooting a sensitive MRI installation.
Join a tight-knit, expert team of 16 where your professional development is treated as a core business investment, not an afterthought.
Competitive Package reflecting the high-level responsibility and specialised nature of the role.

Martin Brothers - AI Engineer

Brisbane - Landscape Construction

About the role

Martin Brothers is a South East Queensland landscape construction and maintenance business with a reputation for delivering quality commercial and residential projects across Brisbane and SEQ. We've been building out AI and automation across the business — automating lead nurturing, procurement workflows, site reporting, and internal operations — and we need someone to help us keep pushing that forward.

What you'll actually be working on

Building and maintaining Zapier workflows across the group — from lead capture to automating processes to financial reporting triggers

Extending and improving our Google Apps Script integrations — automating data flows between Gmail, Google Sheets, Google Drive, and external platforms

Supporting our AI-powered systems.

Helping integrate AI APIs (Claude, OpenAI and similar) into internal tools and workflows

We are trusted by 300+ million people in 100+ countries for our industry-leading security, user fund transparency, trading engine speed, deep liquidity, and an unmatched portfolio of digital-asset products. Binance's Risk Operations team is executing an AI-First transformation — moving from manual case review toward a fully autonomous decision pipeline powered by ROCA (Risk Operations AI Agent). This role sits at the critical human-in-the-loop node: reviewing cases where ROCA's confidence score falls below the auto-decision threshold, generating structured feedback that drives continuous model improvement, and progressively expanding the automation boundary.This is not a traditional fraud analyst role.

Think of it like the shift from manual labor to the steam engine era — the people who thrived weren't the ones who resisted the machine, but those who learned to operate it. Here, you are the operator: you direct the AI, validate its judgment, correct its blind spots, and steer it toward better outcomes. The machine handles the volume; you provide the expertise it can't yet replicate.

Core Review Duties

Review cases escalated by ROCA where confidence score falls below the auto-decision threshold, using AI-assisted signals within the unified case review UI (no multi-system switching)
Make accurate pass / block / escalate decisions on a daily queue spanning KYC Audit, ATO investigation, Reset 2FA, POA Review, and Withdrawal anomaly case types etc.
Escalate Key User cases (Priority ≥ 75) and high-risk patterns to Senior Agent / Team Lead with clear rationale documentation
Maintain SLA compliance (<4h manual resolution time) while sustaining decision accuracy ≥ 98%

AI Feedback & Pipeline Contribution

Label every reviewed case as TP / TN / FP / FN with structured root-cause annotations — this data directly feeds ROCA's continuous learning pipeline
Identify recurring FP/FN patterns and route them through the correct feedback path:
Type A — Feature weight bias → trigger model retraining / fine-tune
Type B — Threshold miscalibration → update Decision Hub rules (with A/B validation)
 Type C — SOP gap or logic error → update SOP (version-tracked)
Actively contribute to improving Precision (>95%) and Recall (>99%) targets by surfacing systematic signal failures to Data and Model teams
Participate in regular AI model calibration sessions: review misclassification batches, validate ground truth, challenge decision thresholds with data

SOP & Knowledge Management

Maintain and update Confluence SOP pages for assigned case types; SOP coverage rate target ≥ 80% (directly impacts ROCA first-pass accuracy)
Flag SOP coverage gaps when ROCA's reasoning chain reveals unhandled scenarios; draft SOP additions for Team Lead review
Onboard and calibrate BPO agents on AI-assisted review workflows; monitor BPO quality against automated QA benchmarks
2+ years in fraud review, risk operations, or KYC/KYB case management
Hands-on experience with ATO (Account Takeover) detection and investigation
Comfort working in AI-augmented workflows: you read model outputs, interpret confidence scores, and make decisions based on AI-generated signals rather than raw data alone
Structured, precise written communication: you can document a root-cause annotation in <3 sentences that an ML engineer can act on
Bilingual English/Mandarin is required to be able to coordinate with overseas partners and stakeholders.
An operator's mindset toward AI tools: you leverage AI signals to make faster, better decisions — you master the tool rather than compete with it
Experience in crypto / Web3 / VASP risk environment; familiarity with blockchain transaction patterns (Sybil attacks, wash trading, mule account indicators)
Exposure to ML model evaluation concepts: Precision, Recall, F1, confusion matrix — you don't need to build models, but you need to speak the language
Familiarity with prompt engineering or LLM evaluation workflows; experience reviewing or testing AI-generated decisions at scale
CAMS, CFE, or equivalent AML / fraud certification
Prior involvement in SOP authoring, QA calibration, or BPO oversight for a risk operations team

Why Binance

• Shape the future with the world’s leading blockchain ecosystem

• Collaborate with world-class talent in a user-centric global organization with a flat structure

• Tackle unique, fast-paced projects with autonomy in an innovative environment

• Thrive in a results-driven workplace with opportunities for career growth and continuous learning

• Competitive salary and company benefits

• Work-from-home arrangement (the arrangement may vary depending on the work nature of the business team)

Job Description


The Role

We’re looking for an AI Engineer to join The Foundry and work at the intersection of client delivery and internal capability building. This is a hands-on engineering role where you’ll design, build, and ship production-grade AI systems for Bilue’s clients across financial services, government, insurance, and retail — while simultaneously contributing to the reference architectures, tooling, and engineering standards that define how AI Labs operates.

This isn’t a research role. It’s not a prompt-engineering role. You’ll be building agentic systems that survive contact with real users, integrating AI into enterprise environments, and setting the quality bar for what “production-grade” means at Bilue.

What You’ll Do

Client Delivery (The Foundry)

Design and build production-grade AI solutions including agentic systems, memory systems, LLM-powered features, and intelligent automation for enterprise clients.

Architect cloud-native AI systems on AWS, Azure, or GCP with robust DevOps/LLMOps pipelines for scalability and reliability.

Integrate AI capabilities into existing enterprise platforms (CRM, ERP, lending systems, claims platforms) through well-designed APIs and data pipelines (leveraging AI of course).

Lead AI technical delivery within cross-functional squads alongside designers, strategists, and client stakeholders — translating complex AI concepts into high-impact outcomes.

Implement responsible AI practices including red-teaming, bias testing, evaluation harnesses, and human-in-the-loop design before anything ships.

Internal Capability (The AI Foundry)

Contribute to Bilue’s reference architectures for common delivery patterns: knowledge/context pipelines, agentic systems, LLM integrations, and evaluation frameworks.

Help define and evolve engineering standards for AI Labs work — code quality, testing approaches, prompt evaluation frameworks, and what “production-grade” means in practice.

Build reusable tooling, accelerators, and “skills” that make the next project faster and more reliable.

Participate in model evaluation, cost optimisation, and architecture reviews that sharpen Bilue’s technical edge.

Share knowledge across the engineering team through documentation, tech talks, and hands-on mentoring.

Qualifications


Experience & Mindset

Demonstrable experience delivering AI or GenAI solutions in a production environment — not just prototypes or demos.

Strong problem-solving ability and the analytical mindset to work with ambiguous, evolving requirements.

The ability to communicate technical concepts clearly to non-technical stakeholders and client executives.

Genuine systems thought leadership — you understand why the system matters, not just how it works.

A collaborative, low-ego approach, working across teams, clients, and contexts with adaptability.

Comfort operating in a fast moving environment where you’ll work across multiple clients and industries.

Strong opinions, weakly held - curious mind with the ability to rapidly evolve when faced with contradictory evidence. 

Technical Skills

Strong programming skills in Python; familiarity with TypeScript or Java is a plus.

Hands-on experience with AI and agentic frameworks — LangChain, AgentCore , LlamaIndex, LangGraph, CrewAI, vector databases, or similar.

Working expertise with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP) 

Solid understanding of data engineering fundamentals — SQL, ETL/ELT, distributed systems, handling large and unstructured datasets.

Experience designing and deploying production AI systems with proper observability, monitoring, and evaluation.

Familiarity with LLMOps practices — prompt versioning, model drift detection, cost monitoring, A/B evaluation.

Understanding of RESTful APIs, microservices, and enterprise integration patterns.

Bonus Points

Experience with the AWS AI/ML stack (Bedrock, AgentCore, Strands, SageMaker, Lambda, Step Functions).

Familiarity with responsible AI frameworks, bias testing tools, or AI governance practices.

Prior consulting or hyper-scaler experience where you’ve delivered for external clients under real constraints.

Contributions to open-source AI projects, AI research, or published technical writing.

Additional Information


Life at Bilue

People-first focus: We’re committed to delivering exceptional outcomes for our clients, but we know it starts with our people. You’ll join a values-led team that’s collaborative, curious, and genuinely cares about doing great work, together.

Connection that counts: From monthly anchor days and team lunches to our annual offsite, we create intentional moments to connect, collaborate, and celebrate. These aren’t just fun perks — they’re part of how we work and grow together.

Flexibility that works: We offer hybrid working, with 1–2 days per week in the office. It’s a balance that gives you the space to do your best work, while still creating time to connect and build strong relationships in person.

Strong internal communities: We actively foster internal communities across tech, design, delivery, and beyond, giving you plenty of chances to connect, share knowledge, and learn from your peers.

Opportunities to grow: We invest in your development with unlimited access to Go1’s learning library and support from our internal performance coach. Whether you want to deepen your technical skills or grow your leadership potential, we’ll back you.

Flat structure, real impact: At Bilue, everyone’s voice matters. Our leadership team is hands-on and approachable, and we operate without unnecessary layers. We keep things open and transparent, and your ideas will be heard, no matter your title.

Job Description


The Role

AI systems don’t fail because of bad models. They fail because of bad libraries, outdated documents cited as current, knowledge that exists but can’t be found, datasets that are technically present but practically untrustworthy.

The AI Information Scientist owns that problem. This is not a traditional data engineering role. It sits at the intersection of data governance, knowledge management, and AI delivery. You will design and maintain the catalogues, metadata schemas, quality frameworks, and lineage structures that every AI system in The Foundry depends on, and you will work directly alongside AI Engineers to connect retrieval systems to the clean, context-aware knowledge stores you’ve built.

Think of it this way: the AI Engineer builds the system that searches the library. You build the library.

What You'll Do

Design and maintain data catalogues for AI projects using platforms such as DataHub, OpenMetadata, Apache Atlas, Collibra, or cloud-native equivalents (AWS Glue Data Catalog, Azure Purview, GCP Dataplex).

Define metadata schemas and taxonomy standards — type, version, jurisdiction, validity period, confidence tier — so retrieval systems know not just what a document is, but when it applies and how much to trust it.

Assess data quality across client and internal assets using tools like Great Expectations, dbt tests, or Soda; flag stale, superseded, or ambiguous records before they reach the AI layer.

Build and maintain data lineage so every AI-generated output can be traced back to its source, version, and validity period — making outputs auditable, not just accurate.

Design automated ingestion workflows and nightly quality checks that keep catalogues current without constant manual intervention.

Partner with The Foundry engineers to connect RAG pipelines and agentic retrieval systems to catalogue APIs, and with The Labs strategists to ma

Qualifications


What We're Looking For

A background in data governance, information management, knowledge management, or records management — with genuine interest in how that work enables AI delivery.

Hands-on experience with at least one data catalogue platform (DataHub, OpenMetadata, Collibra, Alation, Apache Atlas, or a major cloud equivalent) and familiarity with metadata standards such as JSON-LD, Dublin Core, or domain-specific ontologies.

Strong SQL skills; working Python for data profiling, quality scripting, and metadata automation; and comfort with dbt or OpenLineage for lineage tracking.

Understanding of vector databases and RAG architecture — enough to know how metadata quality directly affects retrieval precision.

Experience in regulated or high-stakes data environments where provenance and auditability genuinely matter: financial services, insurance, government, healthcare, or similar.

A collaborative, low-ego disposition. The Data Librarian’s work is structural and often invisible. The glory goes to the AI system. You are fine with that.


Bonus: A formal background in library or information science. Experience with knowledge graphs (Neo4j, RDF, SPARQL). Prior consulting or agency delivery experience.
